items.title: Myanmar junta chief elected vice-president, edging closer to becoming president
items.summary: Min Aung Hlaing, Myanmar's junta chief and leader of the 2021 coup, was elected vice-president by the lower house on Tuesday. The country's parliament also elected another vice-president from a military-aligned party and a third will be chosen by the military. A parliamentary vote to select which of the three will be elevated to president is expected later this week.

items.title: Multiple explosions, airstrikes knock out power in Tehran
items.summary: Multiple explosions rocked Tehran on Tuesday, with news agencies reporting power outages in parts of the Iranian capital. Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says residents have been hearing “airstrikes, followed by the unnerving sound of thundering explosions," he says, noting that airstrikes have been knocking out power in parts of Tehran since Sunday before authorities manage to restore electricity in some neighbourhoods.

items.title: Nadim Houry warns: Israel is no longer just fighting Hezbollah, 'it’s waging war on Lebanon'
items.summary: Jennie Shin is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry. According to Houry, the current conflict in Lebanon reflects not only an immediate military escalation but a deeper structural impasse shaped by decades of unresolved tensions. What we are witnessing is not simply a confrontation between Israel and Hezbollah, but a broader reconfiguration of the Lebanese state’s sovereignty, legitimacy, and strategic options.

items.title: Football: Valiant Black Stars narrowly lose to Germany in Stuttgart
items.summary: Ghana lost 2-1 to Germany in a friendly match in Stuttgart. Ghana will kick off their World Cup campaign on 18 June against Panama, before facing England and Croatia.

items.title: Céline Dion makes comeback with concerts in Paris after six year hiatus
items.summary: Megastar singer Celine Dion on Monday announced her return to performing after a lengthy break prompted by a rare health condition, calling the comeback news revealed on her birthday "the best gift".

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran fires fresh round of missiles, four Israeli soldiers killed in south Lebanon
items.summary: Iran fired new missiles across the Middle East Tuesday, defying US President Donald Trump, who threatened to destroy the country's energy infrastructure unless Tehran accepts a deal to immediately end the war. In southern Lebanon, four Israeli soldiers were killed in clashes with Hezbollah, the Israeli military said. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: NASA starts countdown for first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years
items.summary: NASA kicked off its two-day countdown Monday for its first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years, aiming for a historic launch from Florida’s Kennedy Space Center on Wednesday evening. The Artemis 2 flight, featuring four astronauts — including the first woman, a person of colour and a non-American on a lunar mission — will circle the Moon and test the agency’s new SLS rocket, a key step toward a future lunar base.

items.title: Céline Dion makes her big comeback with concerts in Paris after six-year hiatus
items.summary: On her 58th birthday, Céline Dion announced her return to the stage with 10 concerts in Paris this fall, marking her first major performances since pausing her career for health reasons. The news was highlighted by a Paris-wide ad campaign and a light show on the Eiffel Tower.

items.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iran’s Kharg Island oil hub if no deal reached
items.summary: US President Donald Trump on Monday threatened to destroy Iran’s key oil export hub at Kharg Island and other critical infrastructure unless Tehran swiftly agrees to a deal to end the US-Israeli war, sharply escalating tensions in an already volatile region. The warning has rattled global energy markets and raised fears of a broader conflict, as Iran signals potential retaliation and moves to tighten control over the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: 'The hypocrisy was so stark' expert says, as US reverses course on Cuba oil blockade
items.summary: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Helen Yaffe, Professor of Latin American Political Economy at the University of Glasgow, describes the "stark hypocrisy" of the Trump administration's complaints about Iran blocking free movement on the seas while at the same time weaponising starvation by denying the Cuban people oil. The US has allowed a sanctioned Russian tanker to deliver fuel to the island for humanitarian reasons, saying such decisions would be handled on a ​case-by-case basis.

items.title: En Birmanie, le chef de la junte se rapproche de la présidence
items.summary: Le chef de la junte birmane, Min Aung Hlaing, a été élu vice-président mardi par la chambre basse du Parlement. Une nouvelle étape vers la présidence pour l'auteur du coup d'État de 2021, qui devra abandonner ses fonctions militaires conformément à la Constitution.

translation.title: In Burma, the head of the junta approaches the presidency
translation.summary: The head of the Burmese junta, Min Aung Hlaing, was elected Vice-President on Tuesday by the Lower House of Parliament. A new step towards the presidency for the author of the 2021 coup, who will have to abandon his military functions in accordance with the Constitution.

items.title: Iran : explosions et coupures d'électricité à Téhéran
items.summary: Des explosions et des coupures de courant ont été signalées à Téhéran mardi matin après des frappes israéliennes, selon les médias officiels cités par Siavosh Ghazi, correspondant de France 24 en Iran. À Ispahan, des frappes plus intenses ont visés "des sites militaires", selon Akbar Salehi, un responsable de la sécurité au sein du bureau du gouverneur de la province, cité par l'agence de presse Fars.

translation.title: Iran: Electricity explosions and power cuts in Tehran · Global Voices
translation.summary: Explosions and power cuts were reported in Tehran on Tuesday morning after Israeli strikes, according to official media reports quoted by Siavosh Ghazi, France 24 correspondent in Iran. In Ispahan, more intense strikes targeted "military sites", according to Akbar Salehi, a security officer in the provincial governor's office, quoted by Fars news agency.

items.title: Déserteurs ukrainiens: Sur la ligne de front, "un huis clos à devenir fou"
items.summary: A la Une de la presse, ce mardi 31 mars, les propositions de Volodymyr Zelensky à la Russie. Le président ukrainien se dit prêt à un «cessez-le-feu» pour Pâques et à une «trêve énergétique». L’Ukraine, qui sort à peine de l’hiver le plus rigoureux qu’elle ait connu depuis vingt ans, aggravé par les frappes russes ses installations énergétiques. Un éléphant blanc élu au sénat colombien. Des superfans de Disney adultes. Et la (mauvaise) vie dans les pays machistes.

translation.title: Ukrainian deserters: On the front line, "a closed door to go crazy"
translation.summary: Press coverage, Tuesday 31 March, Volodymyr Zelensky's proposals to Russia. The Ukrainian President says he is ready for a "cease fire" for Easter and an "energy dream". Ukraine, which barely emerged from the most rigorous winter it had experienced in twenty years, aggravated by Russian strikes its energy installations. A white elephant elected to the Colombian Senate. Adult Disney superfans. And (bad) life in the Machist countries.

items.title: En Iran, les Gardiens de la révolution en pleine campagne de recrutement d'enfants-soldats
items.summary: Les Gardiens de la révolution ont lancé une campagne officielle en Iran visant à recruter des enfants dès 12 ans pour ce qu’ils appellent la "défense de la patrie". En pleine guerre, les ONG dénoncent des risques mortels et rappellent que l’utilisation de mineurs constitue un crime de guerre selon le droit international.

translation.title: In Iran, the Revolutionary Guards in the midst of a campaign to recruit child soldiers
translation.summary: The Revolutionary Guards launched an official campaign in Iran to recruit children from the age of 12 for what they call the "defence of the homeland". In the midst of war, NGOs denounce deadly risks and recall that the use of minors constitutes a war crime under international law.

items.title: Céline Dion officialise son grand retour, avec dix concerts à Paris
items.summary: Pour fêter ses 58 ans, Céline Dion a annoncé lundi soir dans une vidéo et via des messages projetés sur la tour Eiffel, une série de dix concerts en septembre et octobre à Paris, officialisant son retour après six ans loin du public. Dix concerts sont prévus du 12 septembre au 14 octobre, à raison de deux concerts par semaine, les samedis et mercredis.

translation.title: Celine Dion formalizes her great return, with ten concerts in Paris
translation.summary: To celebrate her 58th birthday, Celine Dion announced Monday evening in a video and via messages projected on the Eiffel Tower, a series of ten concerts in September and October in Paris, formalizing her return after six years away from the public. Ten concerts are scheduled from September 12 to October 14, with two concerts per week, on Saturdays and Wednesdays.

items.title: Liban : réunion d'urgence à l'ONU après la mort de trois Casques bleus
items.summary: Le Conseil de sécurité de l'ONU doit se réunir en urgence mardi après le décès de trois Casques bleus indonésiens dans le sud du Liban, zone d'affrontements entre Israël et le mouvement pro-iranien Hezbollah.    La France a demandé la tenue de cette réunion, qui débutera à 10 h (14 h GMT), au moment où Israël a ordonné à son armée d'élargir la "zone de sécurité" au Liban où elle mène des frappes massives et une profonde incursion terrestre.

translation.title: Lebanon: United Nations emergency meeting following the death of three peacekeepers
translation.summary: The United Nations Security Council must meet urgently Tuesday after the death of three Indonesian peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, an area of confrontation between Israel and the pro-Iranian Hezbollah movement. France called for this meeting to begin at 10 a.m. (2 p.m. GMT), when Israel ordered its army to expand the "safe zone" in Lebanon, where it carried out massive strikes and a deep ground incursion.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient : les usines de dessalement, la nouvelle cible
items.summary: Le gouvernement koweïtien a déploré lundi une attaque iranienne contre une usine de dessalement qui génère aussi de l'électricité, où un ressortissant indien a été tué. De son côté, l'armée iranienne a démenti, attribuant l'attaque à Israël. Depuis le début de la guerre au Moyen-Orient, des installations similaires ont été ciblées : à Bahreïn, une attaque de drone iranienne avait endommagé une usine de dessalement le 8 mars dernier.

translation.title: War in the Middle East: desalination plants, the new target
translation.summary: The Kuwaiti Government deplored on Monday an Iranian attack on a desalination plant that also generates electricity, where an Indian national was killed. For its part, the Iranian army denied, attributing the attack to Israel. Since the beginning of the Middle East war, similar installations have been targeted: in Bahrain, an Iranian drone attack had damaged a desalination plant on 8 March.

items.title: Football : un Ghana valeureux tombe de justesse face à l'Allemangne
items.summary: Le Ghana s’est incliné (2-1) face à l’Allemagne en match amical à Stuttgart. Le Ghana entamera sa Coupe du monde le 18 juin face au Panama, avant d’affronter l’Angleterre et la Croatie.

translation.title: Football: A Valuable Ghana just falls in front of Germany
translation.summary: Ghana slanted (2-1) against Germany in a friendly match in Stuttgart. Ghana will start its World Cup on June 18 against Panama, before facing England and Croatia.

items.title: Iran : Donald Trump menace d'"anéantir" l'île de Kharg
items.summary: Le président américain Donald Trump, continue de souffler le chaud et le froid sur la guerre au Moyen-Orient, a menacé lundi l'Iran d'anéantir l'île de Kharg, un site pétrolier essentiel pour l'Iran, si le détroit d'Ormuz n'est pas rouvert et si les discussions avec Téhéran, qu'il qualifie de "sérieuses", n'aboutissent pas "rapidement".

translation.title: Iran: Donald Trump threatens to destroy Kharg Island · Global Voices
translation.summary: US President Donald Trump, who continues to blow the heat and the cold over the war in the Middle East, threatened Iran on Monday to destroy the island of Kharg, an essential oil site for Iran, if the Strait of Ormuz is not reopened and if the discussions with Tehran, which he calls "serious", do not end "rapidly".

items.title: Israël : une loi décriée sur la peine de mort pour les Palestiniens adoptée par le Parlement
items.summary: Le Parlement israélien a adopté lundi soir une loi instaurant "la peine de mort pour les terroristes", taillée sur mesure pour ne s'appliquer qu'aux Palestiniens reconnus coupables d'attaques anti-israéliennes meurtrières, l'Autorité palestinienne dénonçant une tentative de "légitimer des exécutions extrajudiciaires". Le Conseil de l'Europe a déploré un "grave recul", une fois la loi adoptée.

translation.title: Israel: A Denied Law on the Death Penalty for Palestinians Adopted by Parliament
translation.summary: On Monday evening, the Israeli Parliament passed a law introducing the "death penalty for terrorists", tailored to apply only to Palestinians convicted of murderous anti-Israeli attacks, with the Palestinian Authority denouncing an attempt to "legitimize extrajudicial executions". The Council of Europe deplored a "serious step backwards", once the law was passed.

items.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

items.summary: Iran said on Monday its ambassador to Lebanon would remain in Beirut despite a Lebanese order to leave, turning a diplomatic reprimand into a broader test of how far the Lebanese state can push back when its decisions collide with the interests of Tehran and Hezbollah.

The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.
